gemms.name: q_proj
gemms.notes: QuantLinear 96->96, INT4 weight, no bias. Preceding: BN + INT4 quant (attn_pre_out).

gemms.name: v_proj
gemms.notes: QuantLinear 96->96, INT4 weight, no bias. TUNED shift baseline-1 (gained +0.47pp on full eval).

gemms.name: qk_head0
gemms.notes: Head 0: slice Q/K along last dim. Both operands are activations (INT4 signed). 1/sqrt(96) attention scale applied in CPU float requant, NOT folded into VTA shift. All 3 heads share per-tensor scales so the shift is identical.

gemms.name: qk_head1
gemms.notes: Head 1: slice Q/K along last dim. Both operands are activations (INT4 signed). 1/sqrt(96) attention scale applied in CPU float requant, NOT folded into VTA shift. All 3 heads share per-tensor scales so the shift is identical.

gemms.name: qk_head2
gemms.notes: Head 2: slice Q/K along last dim. Both operands are activations (INT4 signed). 1/sqrt(96) attention scale applied in CPU float requant, NOT folded into VTA shift. All 3 heads share per-tensor scales so the shift is identical.

gemms.name: av_head0
gemms.notes: Head 0: attn_weights (INT4) × V (INT4). Output quantized by o_projection.input_quant (o_in scale).

gemms.name: av_head1
gemms.notes: Head 1: attn_weights (INT4) × V (INT4). Output quantized by o_projection.input_quant (o_in scale).

gemms.name: av_head2
gemms.notes: Head 2: attn_weights (INT4) × V (INT4). Output quantized by o_projection.input_quant (o_in scale).

gemms.name: o_proj
gemms.notes: QuantLinear 96->96, INT4 weight, no bias. o_projection has no output_quant; next quantizer is the shared residual (attn_residual). TUNED shift baseline-1 (gained +0.69pp full eval — the single biggest lever).

gemms.name: fc1
gemms.notes: QuantLinear 96->384, INT4 weight, has bias. ReLU implicit in clip lower bound (clip [0, 15] = unsigned INT4 post-ReLU). Output range represents post-ReLU values [0, 15 * fc1_out].

gemms.name: fc2
gemms.notes: QuantLinear 384->96, INT4 weight, has bias. Input is unsigned fc1 output [0, 15]; VTA expects signed [-8, 7] so zero-point offset 8 is subtracted from fc1 output and folded into corrected bias = bias_int32 + 8 * sum(W_fc2, axis=1). Next quantizer after fc2 is the shared residual (mlp_residual). TUNED shift baseline-1 (gained +0.43pp full eval).

cpu_ops.name: patch_embedding
cpu_ops.description: Conv2d(2->96, k=(1,16), s=(1,16)) as im2col GEMM + bias + BN + ReLU + unsigned quant + AvgPool
cpu_ops.notes: 1) Quantize input to INT8 signed (scale emb_in). 2) Reshape (B,2,1,1024) -> (B,64,32) via im2col (64 non-overlapping [2,16] patches). 3) INT8 GEMM: acc[B,64,96] = X[B,64,32] @ W[96,32].T; add integer bias. 4) Dequant acc to float * (emb_w * emb_in). 5) BN2d(affine=False) per channel with eps=1e-5. 6) ReLU. 7) Quantize to UNSIGNED INT8 [0, 255] with scale emb_out. 8) AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1,64)) is no-op (already 1x64). 9) Rearrange (B,96,1,64) -> (B,64,96).

cpu_ops.name: positional_encoding
cpu_ops.notes: 1) Dequantize emb output (int8 unsigned * emb_out) to float. 2) Quantize both emb and pos with pos_in scale (INT8 signed). 3) Add in float (or integer at shared scale). 4) Quantize sum with pos_out scale (INT8 signed). Input quant applied identically to BOTH emb output and pos parameter.

cpu_ops.name: bn_attention
cpu_ops.notes: Dequant INT8 positional-output to float, apply BN(affine=False) per channel, requant to INT4 signed [-8,7]. This INT4 is the Q/K/V GEMM input.

cpu_ops.name: attention_scale_and_head_split
cpu_ops.description: Reshape Q/K/V [64,96] -> [3,64,32]; scalar 1/sqrt(96) applied in CPU requant of Q@K^T output
cpu_ops.notes: Reshape Q: einops 'b s (h d) -> b h s d' with h=3,d=32 -> [3, 64, 32]. Reshape K: 'b s (h d) -> b h d s' -> [3, 32, 64] (transposed for matmul). Reshape V: 'b s (h d) -> b h s d' -> [3, 64, 32]. Attention scale 1/sqrt(96)=0.102062 is a float multiply in the CPU requant step following each Q@K^T VTA GEMM; NOT applied as integer multiply on the Q tensor (that would destroy INT4 precision).

cpu_ops.name: softmax
cpu_ops.description: Per-head float softmax between Q@K^T and attn@V VTA calls
cpu_ops.notes: 1) Dequant presoftmax int4 (softmax_in) to float. 2) Stable softmax: subtract row max, exp, divide by sum. 3) Quantize result to INT4 SIGNED [-8, 7] with softmax_out scale. Signed INT4 on [0,1] softmax output wastes half the code space — this is how the model was trained; keep as-is.

cpu_ops.name: head_concat
cpu_ops.notes: einops 'b h s d -> b s (h d)'. Pure reshape, no arithmetic. Input to o_projection (after o_proj.input_quant requant to INT4 o_in scale).

cpu_ops.name: residual_attention
cpu_ops.description: Add O-projection output to positional-encoding skip branch (shared INT4 quantizer)
cpu_ops.notes: The parent QuantIdentity module (enc.1.quant[0]) is applied to BOTH branches with the same learned attn_residual scale. CPU implementation:   skip_int4 = clip(round(skip_float / attn_residual), -8, 7)   main_int4 = o_proj output (already at attn_residual scale, -8..7)   sum_int = main_int4 + skip_int4    # range [-16, 14], not re-clipped   sum_float = sum_int * attn_residual post_norm is Identity (norm_placement=pre-norm).

cpu_ops.name: bn_mlp
cpu_ops.notes: Same pattern as bn_attention. Input is the attention residual sum.

cpu_ops.name: relu_and_unsigned_quant
cpu_ops.description: ReLU on fc1 output, requant to UNSIGNED INT4 [0, 15]
cpu_ops.notes: Mathematically equivalent to: clip(round(acc_float / fc1_out), 0, 15). In Mode E the implementation combines: fc1 GEMM produces int32 acc, VTA coarse-shifts to int8, CPU dequant * w*in then clips to [0, 15] (ReLU is implicit in the lower clip bound). Output is UNSIGNED INT4. fc2 expects SIGNED int4 input so we subtract 8 and fold +8*sum(W_fc2, axis=1) into fc2's bias (see fc2 bias_int32_formula).

cpu_ops.name: residual_mlp
cpu_ops.description: Add fc2 output to attention-block skip branch (shared INT4 quantizer)
cpu_ops.notes: Shared QuantIdentity with mlp_residual scale. skip_int4 = clip(round(skip_float / mlp_residual), -8, 7) main_int4 = fc2 output at mlp_residual scale sum_int = main + skip  # range [-16,14], kept as int or float * mlp_residual

cpu_ops.name: classifier
cpu_ops.description: GAP over 64 positions -> [96], INT8 matmul [1,96]x[24,96].T + float bias -> argmax
cpu_ops.notes: 1) mlp_block_out (int) * mlp_residual -> float (B, 64, 96). 2) Global average pool over 64 temporal positions -> (B, 96). 3) logits = gap @ (W_cls * cls_w).T + cls_bias (float matmul with    dequantized INT8 weights; classifier has NO input quantizer in Brevitas). 4) Optional cls.1 quant: int = clip(round(logits / cls_out), -128, 127). 5) argmax(logits) = argmax(int) — scale-invariant; skip step 4 for prediction. If running the classifier GEMM on VTA-INT8 instead, zero-pad W_cls from [24, 96] to [32, 96] (add 8 zero rows) for BLOCK_OUT=16 alignment.

shift_tuning_rationale.method: Task-4 sensitivity sweep on 20k subset + top-3 full-eval (transformer_sim_results_phase3.json)

notes: All VTA GEMMs use INT4 inputs, INT4 weights, INT8 output (o8 bitstream 1x16_i4w4o8a32).
CPU does precise float requant from int8 coarse-shifted accumulator to int4 between VTA calls.
Tuned shifts use baseline_shifts_before_tuning with -1 on o_proj, v_proj, fc2 (see shift_tuning_rationale).
The 1/sqrt(96) attention scale is folded into CPU float requant of Q@K^T, not VTA shift.
Zero-point offset 8 for unsigned post-ReLU fc2 input is folded into fc2 corrected_bias = bias_int32 + 8*sum(W_fc2, axis=1).
Softmax runs on CPU in float between Q@K^T and attn@V VTA calls.
BN(affine=False) runs on CPU in float before attention and MLP blocks (eps=1e-5).
Classifier runs entirely on CPU — float matmul with dequantized INT8 weight + argmax (Brevitas cls.0 has no input_quant).
Patch embedding conv is INT8 on CPU (outside the 12 VTA INT4 GEMMs).
Mode E == Mode C numerically for this checkpoint (identical accuracy and per-sample predictions confirmed).
Accumulator max was measured on 1000 evenly-spaced eval samples; the tuned shifts have <1% measured clipping but small clipping on unseen outliers is possible.
